_comment: PUBLIC context-overlay golden on the GA4 events replica (single denormalized events table). Writes one overlay entry for the events table, grounded by the local corpus. Requires a writable EG.

run._doc: Runnable CASE: build the GA4 one-events-table replica, then enrich it as a context-overlay entry in your entry group.

golden_facts: holds one row per GA4 event from the Google Merchandise Store -- event-based, not session- or pageview-based
event_name identifies the interaction (e.g. page_view, view_item, add_to_cart, begin_checkout, purchase) and event_timestamp is microseconds since epoch
event_params is a repeated key-value record of event-scoped parameters (e.g. page_location, ga_session_id, currency, value) read by UNNEST + filtering on key
user_pseudo_id is the pseudonymous device identifier (the primary key for counting users); user_id is the business user id used to stitch a signed-in user across devices
nested RECORD columns device, geo, and traffic_source capture per-event context (device category/OS, country/city, acquisition source/medium)
ecommerce holds order-level metrics (e.g. purchase_revenue_in_usd, total_item_quantity) and items is a repeated record of the products in the event (item_id, item_name, price, quantity)
a session is reconstructed at query time from user_pseudo_id plus the ga_session_id event parameter -- there is no separate session or order table
